  40: - `created_at`
  41: - `updated_at`
  42: - `privacy_level`
  43: - `version`
  44: - `lineage`
  45: 
  46: ### 2.2 设计含义
  47: 
  48: - `source_refs`：回指原始对话、原始文件、原始文档、原始工作流结果
  49: - `session_refs`：回指形成该资产的对话会话
  50: - `commit_refs`：回指代码或文档变更事实
  51: - `lineage`：记录该资产由什么资产演化而来
  52: 
  53: ---
  54: 
  55: ## 3. ProductAsset
  56: 
  57: `ProductAsset` 对应创作结果。
  58: 
  59: 它不只代表“代码文件”，而是泛指自然语言创作最终落地的产品性产物。
  60: 
  61: ### 3.1 可包含的对象
  62: 
  63: - 文件
  64: - 模块
  65: - API
  66: - 文档
  67: - 配置
  68: - 数据集
  69: - 发布物
  70: - workflow 运行产物
  71: 
  72: ### 3.2 建议字段
  73: 
  74: - `product_kind`
  75: - `repo_uri`
  76: - `repo_path`
  77: - `branch`
  78: - `commit_range`
  79: - `artifact_format`
  80: - `owner_module`
  81: - `build_state`
  82: - `related_memory_ids`
  83: - `related_skill_ids`
  84: 
  85: ### 3.3 它解决的问题
  86: 
  87: - 一个项目最终产出了什么
  88: - 这些产物位于仓库的什么位置
  89: - 这些产物与哪些对话、决策、技能相关
  90: 
  91: ---
  92: 
  93: ## 4. MemoryAsset
  94: 
  95: `MemoryAsset` 对应创作过程。
  96: 
  97: 它是这次规划的核心，因为“记忆树 + 探针检索”主要服务于它。
  98: 
  99: ### 4.1 可包含的对象
 100: 
 101: - 问题
 102: - 决策
 103: - 约束
 104: - 偏好
 105: - 尝试
 106: - 失败
 107: - 洞见
 108: - 里程碑
 109: - 原始问题演化链
 110: 
 111: ### 4.2 建议字段
 112: 
 113: - `memory_kind`
 114: - `tree_level`
 115: - `anchor_text`
 116: - `raw_refs`
 117: - `parent_id`
 118: - `child_ids`
 119: - `branch_key`
 120: - `evidence_refs`
 121: - `related_product_ids`
 122: - `related_skill_ids`
 123: - `probe_policy`
 124: 
 125: ### 4.3 核心原则
 126: 
 127: - 不是删除原文，而是压缩进入上下文的路径
 128: - 原始内容必须保留
 129: - 树上节点用于导航、压缩、定位、回填
 130: - 召回采用“命中即止”的渐进展开
 131: 
 132: ---
 133: 
 134: ## 5. SkillAsset
 135: 
 136: `SkillAsset` 对应创作能力。
 137: 
 138: 它不是“对话里某句建议”，而是经过复盘后，能重复调用的稳定能力对象。
 139: 
 140: ### 5.1 可包含的对象
 141: 
 142: - 方法论
 143: - checklist
 144: - prompt 模板
 145: - workflow 模板
 146: - 解决模式
 147: - 反模式
 148: - 调试套路
 149: - 评审套路
 150: 
 151: ### 5.2 建议字段
 152: 
 153: - `skill_kind`
 154: - `trigger_conditions`
 155: - `prerequisites`
 156: - `steps`
 157: - `inputs`
 158: - `outputs`
 159: - `success_signals`
 160: - `anti_patterns`
 161: - `evidence_refs`
 162: - `derived_from_memory_ids`
 163: - `applies_to_product_ids`
 164: - `confidence`
 165: 
 166: ### 5.3 它解决的问题
 167: 
 168: - 这次创作最后沉淀出了什么能力
 169: - 这些能力是从哪些对话和结果里提炼出来的
 170: - 下一次遇到类似问题，如何直接复用
 171: 
 172: ---
 173: 
 174: ## 6. 三类资产之间的关系
 175: 
 176: 建议至少支持以下关系：
 177: 
 178: - `MemoryAsset -> ProductAsset`
 179:   - `LEADS_TO`
 180:   - 表示某段过程最终导向了某个产物
 181: 
 182: - `SkillAsset -> ProductAsset`
 183:   - `APPLIES_TO`
 184:   - 表示某个技能可用于某类产物
 185: 
 186: - `SkillAsset -> MemoryAsset`
 187:   - `EXTRACTED_FROM`
 188:   - 表示技能是从某段过程提炼出来的
 189: 
 190: - `MemoryAsset -> MemoryAsset`
 191:   - `EVOLVES_TO`
 192:   - 表示问题、决策、思路的演化
 193: 
 194: - `ProductAsset -> ProductAsset`
 195:   - `DEPENDS_ON`
 196:   - 表示模块、文件、发布物之间的依赖关系
 197: 
 198: ---
 199: 
 200: ## 7. 记忆树最小结构
 201: 
 202: 建议将 `MemoryAsset` 的树形结构明确为四层：
 203: 
 204: - `ConversationLeaf`
 205:   - 最小对话叶子
 206:   - 对应 1-3 轮或一个最小主题片段
 207: 
 208: - `ArchiveBatch`
 209:   - 一组叶子节点的阶段性归档
 210: 
 211: - `MilestoneNode`
 212:   - 一个阶段的核心里程碑
 213: 
 214: - `StageNode`
 215:   - 更高层的阶段主线节点
 216: 
 217: ### 7.1 作用分工
 218: 
 219: - `Leaf`：保留局部具体语境
 220: - `Batch`：做小范围收敛
 221: - `Milestone`：形成阶段主线
 222: - `Stage`：形成长期导航主干
 223: 
 224: ---
 225: 
 226: ## 8. 探针式检索最小接口
 227: 
 228: 建议最小接口为：
 229: 
 230: - `probe_main(query, scope)`
 231:   - 先拿主干节点
 232: 
 233: - `probe_branch(node_id)`
 234:   - 展开某个里程碑下的分支
 235: 
 236: - `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 237:   - 查看具体叶子或小归档
 238: 
 239: - `hydrate_raw(ref)`
 240:   - 必要时回填原始对话或原始文档
 241: 
 242: ### 8.1 检索原则
 243: 
 244: - 先主线，后分支，再叶子，最后原文
 245: - 命中即停
 246: - 不为“可能有用”而全量灌入
 247: - 让上下文负载稳定，不随历史轮数线性膨胀
 248: 
 249: ---
 250: 
 251: ## 9. 与当前代码现实的映射
 252: 
 253: 当前已有对象可以看作未来三类资产模型的前身，而不是错误方向。
 254: 
 255: ### 9.1 现状映射
 256: 
 257: - `memory_unit`
 258:   - 更接近 `MemoryAsset` 的叶子层或结构化记忆单元
 259: 
 260: - `source_asset`
 261:   - 更接近来源/证据层的原始载体对象
 262:   - 当前主要是 transcript source，不等同于未来 `ProductAsset`
 263: 
 264: - `archive_anchor`
 265:   - 更接近 `MemoryAsset` 中的锚点/里程碑节点
 266: 
 267: ### 9.2 这意味着
 268: 
 269: 当前代码基础可以继续沿用，但后续命名、关系、接口应逐步从“记忆对象”升级为“创造资产对象”。
 270: 
 271: ### 9.3 ProductAsset 的当前缺口
 272: 
 273: - 当前代码中还没有与 `ProductAsset` 一一对应的一等运行时对象
 274: - 未来 `ProductAsset` 更适合来自 repo/file/doc/workflow artifact 的独立导入与聚合
 275: - 不应直接把当前 transcript `source_asset` 误当成 `ProductAsset`
 276: 
 277: ### 9.4 与当前 DataLayerAPI 的兼容桥
 278: 
 279: 三类资产模型在短期内不应直接替换当前 `RetrievalReference kind`。
 280: 
 281: - `MemoryAsset`
 282:   - 短期先投影到 `memory_unit + archive_anchor + session context`
 283: 
 284: - `source_asset`
 285:   - 继续作为来源/证据对象存在
 286: 
 287: - `ProductAsset`
 288:   - 短期先通过 repo path / commit context / 文档导入等聚合视图表达
 289: 
 290: - `SkillAsset`
 291:   - 短期先作为从 `MemoryAsset` 派生出来的稳定对象
 292: 
 293: - 当前 `DataLayerAPI`
 294:   - 继续暴露 `session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed`
 295: 
 296: - 后续资产级接口
 297:   - 应优先作为 `DataLayerAPI` 之上的投影层或单独的 `AssetLayerAPI`
 298:   - 不应在没有映射关系的前提下平行创造第二套运行时 kind
 299: 
 300: ---
 301: 
 302: ## 10. 对开源核心边界的启发
 303: 
 304: 从该对象模型出发，开源核心应该至少包含：
 305: 
 306: - 三类资产对象模型
 307: - 统一导入与提炼
 308: - Git/Gitea 回写
 309: - 记忆树生成与探针检索
 310: - FTS / vector / graph / probe 检索层
 311: - 面向上层产品的最小运行时接口
 312: 
 313: 而不应急于直接吸收：
 314: 
 315: - 完整 deer-flow 式 super agent harness
 316: - 重型工作台 UI
 317: - 商业化协作系统
 318: - 市场、计费、SaaS 托管层
 319: 
 320: ---
 321: 
 322: ## 11. 一句话总结
 323: 
 324: OpenMnemo 的下一阶段，不是单纯把“记忆做得更强”，而是要把：
 325: 
 326: **创作结果、创作过程、创作能力**
 327: 
 328: 统一变成可拥有、可检索、可复用、可演化的 Git-native 长期资产。

 295: 
 296: - 后续资产级接口
 297:   - 应优先作为 `DataLayerAPI` 之上的投影层或单独的 `AssetLayerAPI`
 298:   - 不应在没有映射关系的前提下平行创造第二套运行时 kind
 299: 
 300: ---
 301: 
 302: ## 10. 对开源核心边界的启发
 303: 
 304: 从该对象模型出发，开源核心应该至少包含：
 305: 
 306: - 三类资产对象模型
 307: - 统一导入与提炼
 308: - Git/Gitea 回写
 309: - 记忆树生成与探针检索
 310: - FTS / vector / graph / probe 检索层
 311: - 面向上层产品的最小运行时接口
 312: 
 313: 而不应急于直接吸收：
 314: 
 315: - 完整 deer-flow 式 super agent harness
 316: - 重型工作台 UI
 317: - 商业化协作系统
 318: - 市场、计费、SaaS 托管层
 319: 
 320: ---
 321: 
 322: ## 11. 一句话总结
 323: 
 324: OpenMnemo 的下一阶段，不是单纯把“记忆做得更强”，而是要把：
 325: 
 326: **创作结果、创作过程、创作能力**
 327: 
 328: 统一变成可拥有、可检索、可复用、可演化的 Git-native 长期资产。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

 604: ## 9. 一句话总结
 605: 
 606: OpenMnemo 的记忆压缩架构，不应是“把越来越多的历史缩成越来越短的摘要”，而应是：

 608: **Session Tree 管单会话，Repo Tree 管单仓库，Global Memory Forest 管所有仓库；Probe Pipeline 管递进展开，Graph 管跨域关联，Git/Gitea 管事实与版本，Budget Profiles 管不同模型下的上下文压缩强度。**

events.text:
**Findings**

1. `Medium` 探针接口已经分叉成了三套语义，不再是同一层抽象。Session Tree 写的是 `probe_main(session_id)`，Repo Tree 又变成 `probe_repo_main(repo_id)`，资产模型文档则写成 `probe_main(query, scope)`。这不是小的命名差异，而是把“按对象导航”和“按查询检索”混到了一起；如果现在不统一，后面实现 runtime、tool schema、LLM 调用路由时一定会漂。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:176](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L176) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:287](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L287) [创造资产内核对象模型_2026-03-24.md:230](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/创造资产内核对象模型_2026-03-24.md#L230)

2. `Medium` Global Memory Forest 还没有彻底讲清“物理节点”和“scope 视图”的关系。示例里是严格的 `ScopeRoot -> RepoRoot` 单归属结构，但后文又明确说分身拿到的是按 `scope / persona / privacy` 裁剪后的“记忆树视图”，而跨 scope 关联交给 Graph。如果按现在的例子直接实现，后面做 `shared`、跨 scope 授权、跨仓 skill 迁移时，很容易出现 repo 重复挂载或权限判断失真。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:323](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L323) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:355](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L355) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:371](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L371)

3. `Medium` todo 状态已经落后于设计现状。`todo_v003` 还把“形成 `Session Tree / Repo Tree / Global Memory Forest / Budget Profiles` 的统一设计稿”标成未完成，但统一设计稿其实已经单独成文。这个会直接影响下一轮执行，因为别人会误以为概念设计还没收口。参考：[todo_v003_001_20260324.md:17](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v003_001_20260324.md#L17) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:608](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L608)

4. `Low/Medium` Session Tree 的压缩触发条件仍然偏“代码会话”，对纯对话记忆场景不够稳。现在“主题漂移触发”写的是“文件集、关键词、意图明显变化”，但你们要覆盖的是创作交流、人生记忆、技能沉淀，这类会话可能根本没有稳定文件集。这样一来，非代码对话的分叉和压缩时机就会不稳定。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:147](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L147)

两轮 review 下来，当前能明确算“文档级 bug”的有 4 个，没有发现方向性推翻的问题，但第 1 和第 2 个要优先修，不然一进入实现阶段就会开始分叉。第 3 和第 4 个属于执行层和适配层 bug，也建议尽快收口。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把这 4 个点改进成一版 `v2` 规划文档，只收口术语、接口和树模型，不动产品代码。

1. `Medium` 探针接口已经分叉成了三套语义，不再是同一层抽象。Session Tree 写的是 `probe_main(session_id)`，Repo Tree 又变成 `probe_repo_main(repo_id)`，资产模型文档则写成 `probe_main(query, scope)`。这不是小的命名差异，而是把“按对象导航”和“按查询检索”混到了一起；如果现在不统一，后面实现 runtime、tool schema、LLM 调用路由时一定会漂。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:176](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L176) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:287](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L287) [创造资产内核对象模型_2026-03-24.md:230](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/创造资产内核对象模型_2026-03-24.md#L230)

2. `Medium` Global Memory Forest 还没有彻底讲清“物理节点”和“scope 视图”的关系。示例里是严格的 `ScopeRoot -> RepoRoot` 单归属结构，但后文又明确说分身拿到的是按 `scope / persona / privacy` 裁剪后的“记忆树视图”，而跨 scope 关联交给 Graph。如果按现在的例子直接实现，后面做 `shared`、跨 scope 授权、跨仓 skill 迁移时，很容易出现 repo 重复挂载或权限判断失真。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:323](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L323) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:355](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L355) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:371](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L371)

3. `Medium` todo 状态已经落后于设计现状。`todo_v003` 还把“形成 `Session Tree / Repo Tree / Global Memory Forest / Budget Profiles` 的统一设计稿”标成未完成，但统一设计稿其实已经单独成文。这个会直接影响下一轮执行，因为别人会误以为概念设计还没收口。参考：[todo_v003_001_20260324.md:17](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v003_001_20260324.md#L17) [OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:608](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L608)

4. `Low/Medium` Session Tree 的压缩触发条件仍然偏“代码会话”，对纯对话记忆场景不够稳。现在“主题漂移触发”写的是“文件集、关键词、意图明显变化”，但你们要覆盖的是创作交流、人生记忆、技能沉淀，这类会话可能根本没有稳定文件集。这样一来，非代码对话的分叉和压缩时机就会不稳定。参考：[OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md:147](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_记忆树与上下文压缩架构_v1_2026-03-24.md#L147)

两轮 review 下来，当前能明确算“文档级 bug”的有 4 个，没有发现方向性推翻的问题，但第 1 和第 2 个要优先修，不然一进入实现阶段就会开始分叉。第 3 和第 4 个属于执行层和适配层 bug，也建议尽快收口。

如果你愿意，我下一步可以直接把这 4 个点改进成一版 `v2` 规划文档，只收口术语、接口和树模型，不动产品代码。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js 鈮?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only 鈥?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js 鈮?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js 鈮?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only 鈥?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js 鈮?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

 142:

 144:   - 例如累计 2-4 个 user turn
 145: - `token 触发`

 147: - `主题漂移触发`

 149: - `工具洪峰触发`
 150:   - 出现大段代码、日志、终端输出
 151: 
 152: ### 3.5 Session Tree 的核心字段
 153: 
 154: 建议每个节点至少有：
 155: 
 156: - `id`
 157: - `session_id`
 158: - `node_type`
 159: - `parent_id`
 160: - `child_ids`
 161: - `title`
 162: - `summary`
 163: - `anchor_text`
 164: - `keywords`
 165: - `scope_id`
 166: - `evidence_refs`
 167: - `raw_refs`
 168: - `created_at`
 169: - `updated_at`
 170: - `weight`
 171: 
 172: ### 3.6 Session Tree 的 probe 路径
 173: 
 174: 单会话检索建议固定为：
 175: 
 176: 1. `probe_main(session_id)`
 177: 2. `probe_branch(milestone_id | stage_id)`
 178: 3. `probe_leaf(batch_id | leaf_id)`
 179: 4. `hydrate_raw(raw_ref)`
 180: 
 181: ### 3.7 Session 层停止规则
 182: 
 183: - 主线节点已足够回答，停止
 184: - 分支节点已补足细节，停止
 185: - 叶子节点已提供可验证证据，停止
 186: - 必须核对原文时才回填 raw
 187: 
 188: ---
 189: 
 190: ## 4. Repo Tree：单一仓库的记忆树
 283: ### 4.6 Repo Tree 的 probe 路径
 284: 
 285: 建议单仓库检索时按以下顺序：
 286: 
 287: 1. `probe_repo_main(repo_id)`
 288: 2. `probe_workstream(branch_id)`
 289: 3. `probe_milestone(node_id)`
 290: 4. `probe_session_leaf(node_id)`
 291: 5. `hydrate_raw(ref)`
 292: 
 293: ### 4.7 Repo Tree 典型回答场景
 294: 
 295: Repo Tree 应能稳定回答：
 296: 
 297: - 为什么这个仓库走到今天这条路
 298: - 某条 workstream 曾经踩过哪些坑
 299: - 当前阶段有哪些已定决策
 300: - 哪些实践已经上升为 skill
 301: 
 302: ---
 303: 
 304: ## 5. Global Memory Forest：所有仓库的全局记忆森林
 305: 
 306: ### 5.1 为什么不是“一棵全局树”
 307: 
 308: 所有仓库之上不建议强行做成一棵树，因为：
 309: 
 310: - 一个仓库可能同时属于多个主题
 311: - 一个 skill 会跨多个仓库
 312: - 一个决策可能跨工作、个人、家庭
 313: - 权限隔离与分身授权难以落到单树上
 314: 
 315: 因此，全局层更适合：
 316: 
 317: **森林负责压缩导航，图负责跨域关联。**
 318: 
 319: ### 5.2 建议结构
 320: 
 321: ```text
 322: GlobalForest
 323: ├── ScopeRoot(work)
 324: │   ├── RepoRoot(openmnemo)
 325: │   ├── RepoRoot(client-a)
 326: │   └── RepoRoot(content-studio)
 327: ├── ScopeRoot(personal)
 328: │   ├── RepoRoot(life-os)
 329: │   └── RepoRoot(reading-notes)
 330: ├── ScopeRoot(family)
 331: └── ScopeRoot(shared)
 332: ```
 333: 
 334: ### 5.3 Global 层的核心作用
 335: 
 353: ### 5.5 Global 层与 Graph Layer 的关系
 354: 
 355: Global Forest 不是 Graph 的替代品。
 356: 
 357: 建议分工：
 358: 
 359: - `Forest`
 360:   - 压缩导航
 361:   - 分区隔离
 362:   - 长期主线
 363: 
 364: - `Graph`
 365:   - 跨 scope 关联
 366:   - 跨 repo skill 迁移
 367:   - 跨主题灵感串联
 368: 
 369: ### 5.6 分身在 Global 层的形态
 370: 
 371: “每个分身都有自己独立的记忆树”应解释为：
 372: 
 373: **每个分身拥有自己独立的记忆树视图。**
 374: 
 375: 视图由以下维度裁剪：
 376: 
 377: - `scope`
 378: - `project`
 379: - `persona`
 380: - `privacy_level`
 381: - `allow_cross_scope`
 382: 
 383: 例如：
 384: 
 385: - 工作分身默认只能看 `work`
 386: - 家庭分身默认只能看 `family`
 387: - 战略分身可看 `work + shared`
 388: - 只有显式授权时才允许跨 scope 引入敏感内容
 389: 
 390: ---
 426: - 是否已覆盖用户问题中的核心实体/时间/项目
 427: 
 428: ### 6.4 兜底入口
 429: 
 430: 当 tree probe 不足以命中时，可开放：
 431: 
 432: - `full_search(query, scope)`
 433: 
 434: 它不直接进回答，而是：

# 2026-03-24 13:15 Session Log

---
Time: 2026-03-24 13:15
User: Continued the planning discussion after confirming the higher-level direction around “创造资产内核”.
Assistant: Reframed OpenMnemo from a pure memory base into an open creation-asset kernel. Confirmed the three primary asset classes as 创作结果 / 创作过程 / 创作能力, clarified that deer-flow-like systems should be treated as possible upper-layer product forms rather than the kernel itself, and proposed that the open-source core should focus on asset modeling, Git/Gitea-native storage, memory-tree navigation, probe-style retrieval, and minimal runtime interfaces. Then wrote a new goal version, bound todo, and a dedicated knowledge note that defines `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset`, their shared `BaseAsset` fields, the minimal memory-tree node hierarchy, and the probe retrieval interface.

---
Time: 2026-03-24 13:26
User: Requested two review passes over the newly written planning documents and then approved fixing the document issues.
Assistant: Performed two documentation review passes. The first pass found a planning continuity issue where the new active todo had dropped still-open implementation work from the previous active todo, plus a positioning conflict where the earlier unified Doubao knowledge note still framed OpenMnemo as a “个人 AI 记忆底座” instead of the newly confirmed “创造资产内核”. The second pass checked the new asset model against the current codebase and found two more design risks: `source_asset` had been mapped too loosely toward `ProductAsset`, and the documents had not yet explained how the new `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` model should coexist with the current `DataLayerAPI` runtime kinds (`session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed`). Updated the goal, todo, and knowledge docs to carry forward still-open implementation work, align the north-star wording, narrow the `source_asset` mapping, and add a compatibility-bridge note that keeps the current data-layer API as the runtime surface while the new asset model lands first as a projection layer.

---
Time: 2026-03-24 13:35
User: Confirmed continuing the discussion on memory and context compression, and explicitly asked for a plan covering a single-session memory tree, a per-repository memory tree, an all-repositories memory tree, and compression planning for different model context lengths.
Assistant: Re-read the two referenced Doubao documents and distilled the usable theory into a concrete architectural proposal. Confirmed that the viable direction is not “compress the facts” but “compress the path into context”, then formalized a three-tier navigation structure: `Session Tree` for one conversation, `Repo Tree` for one repository, and `Global Memory Forest` across repositories. Clarified that Git/Gitea should remain the fact/version layer rather than be treated as the runtime memory tree itself, and that “each avatar has its own memory tree” should be implemented as isolated tree views rather than physical full copies. Added a dedicated knowledge note defining the four core pieces of the compression architecture: `Session Tree`, `Repo Tree`, `Global Memory Forest`, and `Model Budget Profiles`, along with the associated `Probe Pipeline`.

  71: ## 3. Session Tree：单一会话的记忆树
  72: 
  73: ### 3.1 目标
  74: 
  75: Session Tree 的目标是：
  76: 
  77: - 让超长对话不会线性膨胀
  78: - 让当前模型始终只读会话中的最小必要片段
  79: - 让会话内部的主线、分支、细节都可被追踪
  80: 
  81: ### 3.2 最小层级
  82: 
  83: 建议固定为四层：
  84: 
  85: ```text
  86: SessionRoot
  87: ├── StageNode
  88: │   ├── MilestoneNode
  89: │   │   ├── ArchiveBatch
  90: │   │   │   ├── ConversationLeaf
  91: │   │   │   ├── ConversationLeaf
  92: │   │   │   └── ConversationLeaf
  93: ```
  94: 
  95: ### 3.3 各层职责
  96: 
  97: #### `ConversationLeaf`
  98: 
  99: 最小语义单元，不建议死板绑定“每 3 轮”。
 100: 
 101: 更合理的触发单位：
 102: 
 103: - 1-3 个 user turn
 104: - 一个明确主题片段
 105: - 一次大型工具输出
 106: - 一次明确的结论或转折
 107: 
 108: #### `ArchiveBatch`
 109: 
 110: 小范围收敛节点。
 111: 
 112: 职责：
 113: 
 114: - 聚合若干相近 leaf
 115: - 形成局部锚点
 116: - 保留局部线索与局部主题
 117: 
 118: #### `MilestoneNode`
 119: 
 120: 阶段性里程碑。
 121: 
 122: 建议只记录高价值内容：
 123: 
 124: - 形成了什么结论
 125: - 做了什么决策
 126: - 确立了什么约束
 127: - 暂时未解决什么问题
 128: 
 129: #### `StageNode`
 130: 
 131: 更高层的会话主线节点。
 132: 
 133: 职责：
 134: 
 135: - 标识会话当前阶段
 560: 
 561: - `memory_unit`
 562:   - 更接近 Session Tree 的 leaf 层素材
 563: 
 564: - `archive_anchor`
 565:   - 更接近 Session Tree / Repo Tree 的锚点层素材
 566: 
 567: - `source_asset`
 568:   - 更接近来源/证据层对象
 569: 
 570: - `session`
 571:   - 更接近当前的会话级容器对象
 572: 
 573: ### 8.2 短期不要做的事
 574: 
 575: - 不要立刻用新树模型替换现有 `DataLayerAPI`
 576: - 不要把 Git commit 树直接当运行时树
 577: - 不要把所有仓库强行塞成一棵树
 578: - 不要为每个分身物理复制全量记忆
 579: 
 580: ### 8.3 短期建议路线
 581: 
 582: #### Phase A
 583: 
 584: - 先把 Session Tree 落成正式数据结构
 585: - 让 `memory_unit + archive_anchor` 能映射到 `leaf / batch / milestone`
 586: 
 587: #### Phase B
 588: 
 589: - 在 Repo 级别引入 workstream / repo milestone
 590: - 让 repo 查询不再只靠 session 聚合

 141: 不要只按固定轮次压缩，建议组合四类信号：
 142: 
 143: - `轮次触发`
 144:   - 例如累计 2-4 个 user turn
 145: - `token 触发`
 146:   - 当前活跃上下文接近预算上限
 147: - `主题漂移触发`
 148:   - 文件集、关键词、意图、人物/项目/任务焦点明显变化
 149:   - 对纯对话场景，可按目标、人物、事件、时间段的切换判定
 150: - `工具洪峰触发`
 151:   - 出现大段代码、日志、终端输出
 152: 
 153: ### 3.5 Session Tree 的核心字段
 154: 
 155: 建议每个节点至少有：
 156: 
 157: - `id`
 158: - `session_id`
 159: - `node_type`
 160: - `parent_id`
 161: - `child_ids`
 162: - `title`
 163: - `summary`
 164: - `anchor_text`
 165: - `keywords`
 166: - `scope_id`
 167: - `evidence_refs`
 168: - `raw_refs`
 169: - `created_at`
 170: - `updated_at`
 171: - `weight`
 172: 
 173: ### 3.6 Session Tree 的 probe 路径
 174: 
 175: 单会话检索建议复用统一最小接口，不再为 session 层单独发明一套公开 API：
 176: 
 177: 1. `probe_main(query, session_id)`
 178: 2. `probe_branch(node_id, query?)`
 179: 3. `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 180: 4. `hydrate_raw(raw_ref)`
 181: 
 182: 其中 `session_id` 是 `root_ref`，`milestone / stage` 与 `batch / leaf` 通过 `node_type` 区分。
 183: 
 283: - ProductAsset 候选对象
 284: - SkillAsset 候选对象
 285: 
 286: ### 4.6 Repo Tree 的 probe 路径
 287: 
 288: 建议单仓库检索时也复用同一套最小接口：
 289: 
 290: 1. `probe_main(query, repo_id)`
 291: 2. `probe_branch(node_id, query?)`
 292: 3. `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 293: 4. `hydrate_raw(ref)`
 294: 
 295: 其中 `workstream / repo milestone / session leaf / artifact leaf` 仍通过 `node_type` 区分，不再额外暴露 `probe_repo_main / probe_workstream` 这类平行公开接口。
 296: 
 297: ### 4.7 Repo Tree 典型回答场景
 298: 
 299: Repo Tree 应能稳定回答：
 300: 
 301: - 为什么这个仓库走到今天这条路
 302: - 某条 workstream 曾经踩过哪些坑
 303: - 当前阶段有哪些已定决策
 304: - 哪些实践已经上升为 skill
 305: 
 306: ---
 307: 
 308: ## 5. Global Memory Forest：所有仓库的全局记忆森林
 309: 
 310: ### 5.1 为什么不是“一棵全局树”
 311: 
 312: 所有仓库之上不建议强行做成一棵树，因为：
 313: 
 314: - 一个仓库可能同时属于多个主题
 315: - 一个 skill 会跨多个仓库
 316: - 一个决策可能跨工作、个人、家庭
 317: - 权限隔离与分身授权难以落到单树上
 318: 
 319: 因此，全局层更适合：
 320: 
 321: **森林负责压缩导航，图负责跨域关联。**
 322: 
 323: ### 5.2 建议结构
 324: 
 325: ```text
 326: GlobalForest
 327: ├── RepoIndex
 328: │   ├── RepoRoot(openmnemo)
 329: │   ├── RepoRoot(client-a)
 330: │   ├── RepoRoot(content-studio)
 331: │   ├── RepoRoot(life-os)
 332: │   └── RepoRoot(reading-notes)
 333: ├── ScopeView(work)
 334: │   ├── Mount(openmnemo)
 335: │   ├── Mount(client-a)
 336: │   └── Mount(content-studio)
 337: ├── ScopeView(personal)
 338: │   ├── Mount(life-os)
 339: │   └── Mount(reading-notes)
 340: ├── ScopeView(family)
 341: └── ScopeView(shared)
 342:     └── Mount(openmnemo: shared skill / public milestone)
 343: ```
 344: 
 345: 说明：
 346: 
 347: - `RepoRoot` 是底层唯一身份
 348: - `ScopeView` 只保存挂载关系或投影视图，不复制 repo 原文
 349: - 同一 repo 可同时挂到多个 scope，是否可见由权限与视图层裁剪决定
 350: 
 446: 
 447: 当 tree probe 不足以命中时，可开放：
 448: 
 449: - `full_search(query, scope_id)`
 450: 
 451: 它属于兜底检索，而不是 tree 的最小公开接口。
 452: 
 453: 它不直接进回答，而是：
 454:

 User: Confirmed continuing the discussion on memory and context compression, and explicitly asked for a plan covering a single-session memory tree, a per-repository memory tree, an all-repositories memory tree, and compression planning for different model context lengths.
 Assistant: Re-read the two referenced Doubao documents and distilled the usable theory into a concrete architectural proposal. Confirmed that the viable direction is not “compress the facts” but “compress the path into context”, then formalized a three-tier navigation structure: `Session Tree` for one conversation, `Repo Tree` for one repository, and `Global Memory Forest` across repositories. Clarified that Git/Gitea should remain the fact/version layer rather than be treated as the runtime memory tree itself, and that “each avatar has its own memory tree” should be implemented as isolated tree views rather than physical full copies. Added a dedicated knowledge note defining the four core pieces of the compression architecture: `Session Tree`, `Repo Tree`, `Global Memory Forest`, and `Model Budget Profiles`, along with the associated `Probe Pipeline`.
+
+---
+Time: 2026-03-24 14:05
+User: Asked to fix the document issues found during the double review.
+Assistant: Repaired the planning documents without touching product code. Unified the public probe contract across the knowledge docs around `probe_main(query, root_ref) / probe_branch(node_id, query?) / probe_leaf(node_id) / hydrate_raw`, clarified that repo- or workstream-specific probe names should only remain internal wrappers, upgraded the Session Tree trigger rules so they also work for pure dialogue sessions rather than only code-heavy sessions, and rewrote the `Global Memory Forest` example from a single-parent `ScopeRoot -> RepoRoot` tree into a `RepoIndex + ScopeView + Mount` model so one repo can be projected into multiple scopes without duplicating raw memory. Also synchronized the active todo by marking the completed design items done and adding a new follow-up task to convert the unified design into implementation-level schema, storage mapping, and tool contracts.
*** End Patch

---
Time: 2026-03-24 13:15
User: Continued the planning discussion after confirming the higher-level direction around “创造资产内核”.
Assistant: Reframed OpenMnemo from a pure memory base into an open creation-asset kernel. Confirmed the three primary asset classes as 创作结果 / 创作过程 / 创作能力, clarified that deer-flow-like systems should be treated as possible upper-layer product forms rather than the kernel itself, and proposed that the open-source core should focus on asset modeling, Git/Gitea-native storage, memory-tree navigation, probe-style retrieval, and minimal runtime interfaces. Then wrote a new goal version, bound todo, and a dedicated knowledge note that defines `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset`, their shared `BaseAsset` fields, the minimal memory-tree node hierarchy, and the probe retrieval interface.

---
Time: 2026-03-24 13:26
User: Requested two review passes over the newly written planning documents and then approved fixing the document issues.
Assistant: Performed two documentation review passes. The first pass found a planning continuity issue where the new active todo had dropped still-open implementation work from the previous active todo, plus a positioning conflict where the earlier unified Doubao knowledge note still framed OpenMnemo as a “个人 AI 记忆底座” instead of the newly confirmed “创造资产内核”. The second pass checked the new asset model against the current codebase and found two more design risks: `source_asset` had been mapped too loosely toward `ProductAsset`, and the documents had not yet explained how the new `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` model should coexist with the current `DataLayerAPI` runtime kinds (`session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed`). Updated the goal, todo, and knowledge docs to carry forward still-open implementation work, align the north-star wording, narrow the `source_asset` mapping, and add a compatibility-bridge note that keeps the current data-layer API as the runtime surface while the new asset model lands first as a projection layer.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

events.output:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.6 seconds
Output:
 141: 不要只按固定轮次压缩，建议组合四类信号：
 142: 
 143: - `轮次触发`
 144:   - 例如累计 2-4 个 user turn
 145: - `token 触发`
 146:   - 当前活跃上下文接近预算上限
 147: - `主题漂移触发`
 148:   - 文件集、关键词、意图、人物/项目/任务焦点明显变化
 149:   - 对纯对话场景，可按目标、人物、事件、时间段的切换判定
 150: - `工具洪峰触发`
 151:   - 出现大段代码、日志、终端输出
 152: 
 153: ### 3.5 Session Tree 的核心字段
 154: 
 155: 建议每个节点至少有：
 156: 
 157: - `id`
 158: - `session_id`
 159: - `node_type`
 160: - `parent_id`
 161: - `child_ids`
 162: - `title`
 163: - `summary`
 164: - `anchor_text`
 165: - `keywords`
 166: - `scope_id`
 167: - `evidence_refs`
 168: - `raw_refs`
 169: - `created_at`
 170: - `updated_at`
 171: - `weight`
 172: 
 173: ### 3.6 Session Tree 的 probe 路径
 174: 
 175: 单会话检索建议复用统一最小接口，不再为 session 层单独发明一套公开 API：
 176: 
 177: 1. `probe_main(query, session_id)`
 178: 2. `probe_branch(node_id, query?)`
 179: 3. `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 180: 4. `hydrate_raw(raw_ref)`
 181: 
 182: 其中 `session_id` 是 `root_ref`，`milestone / stage` 与 `batch / leaf` 通过 `node_type` 区分。
 183: 
 286: ### 4.6 Repo Tree 的 probe 路径
 287: 
 288: 建议单仓库检索时也复用同一套最小接口：
 289: 
 290: 1. `probe_main(query, repo_id)`
 291: 2. `probe_branch(node_id, query?)`
 292: 3. `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 293: 4. `hydrate_raw(ref)`
 294: 
 295: 其中 `workstream / repo milestone / session leaf / artifact leaf` 仍通过 `node_type` 区分，不再额外暴露 `probe_repo_main / probe_workstream` 这类平行公开接口。
 296: 
 297: ### 4.7 Repo Tree 典型回答场景
 298: 
 299: Repo Tree 应能稳定回答：
 300: 
 301: - 为什么这个仓库走到今天这条路
 302: - 某条 workstream 曾经踩过哪些坑
 303: - 当前阶段有哪些已定决策
 304: - 哪些实践已经上升为 skill
 305: 
 306: ---
 307: 
 308: ## 5. Global Memory Forest：所有仓库的全局记忆森林
 309: 
 310: ### 5.1 为什么不是“一棵全局树”
 311: 
 312: 所有仓库之上不建议强行做成一棵树，因为：
 313: 
 314: - 一个仓库可能同时属于多个主题
 315: - 一个 skill 会跨多个仓库
 316: - 一个决策可能跨工作、个人、家庭
 317: - 权限隔离与分身授权难以落到单树上
 318: 
 319: 因此，全局层更适合：
 320: 
 321: **森林负责压缩导航，图负责跨域关联。**
 322: 
 323: ### 5.2 建议结构
 324: 
 325: ```text
 326: GlobalForest
 327: ├── RepoIndex
 328: │   ├── RepoRoot(openmnemo)
 329: │   ├── RepoRoot(client-a)
 330: │   ├── RepoRoot(content-studio)
 331: │   ├── RepoRoot(life-os)
 332: │   └── RepoRoot(reading-notes)
 333: ├── ScopeView(work)
 334: │   ├── Mount(openmnemo)
 335: │   ├── Mount(client-a)
 336: │   └── Mount(content-studio)
 337: ├── ScopeView(personal)
 338: │   ├── Mount(life-os)
 339: │   └── Mount(reading-notes)
 340: ├── ScopeView(family)
 341: └── ScopeView(shared)
 342:     └── Mount(openmnemo: shared skill / public milestone)
 343: ```
 344: 
 345: 说明：
 346: 
 347: - `RepoRoot` 是底层唯一身份
 348: - `ScopeView` 只保存挂载关系或投影视图，不复制 repo 原文
 349: - 同一 repo 可同时挂到多个 scope，是否可见由权限与视图层裁剪决定
 389: 
 390: **每个分身拥有自己独立的记忆树视图。**
 391: 
 392: 视图由以下维度裁剪：
 393: 
 394: - `scope`
 395: - `project`
 396: - `persona`
 397: - `privacy_level`
 398: - `allow_cross_scope`
 399: 
 400: 例如：
 401: 
 402: - 工作分身默认只能看 `work`
 403: - 家庭分身默认只能看 `family`
 404: - 战略分身可看 `work + shared`
 405: - 只有显式授权时才允许跨 scope 引入敏感内容
 406: 
 407: ---
 408: 
 409: ## 6. Probe Pipeline：探针式递进检索
 410: 
 411: ### 6.1 总体目标
 412: 
 413: Probe Pipeline 负责控制：
 414: 
 415: - 从哪一层开始找
 416: - 要不要继续往下钻
 417: - 什么时候停止
 418: - 什么时候需要回填原文
 419: 
 420: ### 6.2 推荐执行顺序
 421: 
 422: ```text
 423: Intent Parse
 424: → Scope Select
 425: → Tree Level Select
 426: → probe_main
 427: → probe_branch
 428: → probe_leaf
 429: → hydrate_raw
 430: → answer
 431: ```
 432: 
 433: ### 6.3 停止判断
 434: 
 435: 建议采用“机制优先、模型兜底”的原则。
 436: 
 437: 刚性条件至少包括：
 438: 
 439: - 命中层级
 440: - 信息完整度
 441: - 事实来源是否存在
 442: - 当前预算是否触线
 443: - 是否已覆盖用户问题中的核心实体/时间/项目
 444: 
 445: ### 6.4 兜底入口
 446: 
 447: 当 tree probe 不足以命中时，可开放：
 448: 
 449: - `full_search(query, scope_id)`
 450: 
 451: 它属于兜底检索，而不是 tree 的最小公开接口。
 600: 
 601: #### Phase A
 602: 
 603: - 先把 Session Tree 落成正式数据结构
 604: - 让 `memory_unit + archive_anchor` 能映射到 `leaf / batch / milestone`
 605: 
 606: #### Phase B
 607: 
 608: - 在 Repo 级别引入 workstream / repo milestone

 226: ## 8. 探针式检索最小接口
 227: 
 228: 建议最小接口为：
 229: 
 230: - `probe_main(query, root_ref)`
 231:   - 先拿主干节点，`root_ref` 可为 `session_id / repo_id / scope_id`
 232: 
 233: - `probe_branch(node_id, query?)`
 234:   - 展开某个里程碑、stage、workstream 下的分支
 235: 
 236: - `probe_leaf(node_id)`
 237:   - 查看具体叶子、小归档或 artifact leaf
 238: 
 239: - `hydrate_raw(ref)`
 240:   - 必要时回填原始对话或原始文档
 241: 
 242: - `probe_repo_main / probe_workstream`
 243:   - 可作为内部 wrapper，但不建议成为公共最小接口
 289:

 292:

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

metadata.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

# Project Goal v003

## Metadata
- Version: v003
- Created: 2026-03-24
- Previous: goal_v002_20260319.md
- Updated: 2026-03-24

## North Star

OpenMnemo 应从“AI 对话记忆底座”进一步明确升级为：

**一个面向自然语言创作时代的开源创造资产内核。**

它负责把用户在自然语言驱动的创作活动中沉淀出来的三类核心资产统一收口到 Git/Gitea 体系中：

- 创作结果
- 创作过程
- 创作能力

并将这些资产组织为：

- 可版本化
- 可检索
- 可回溯
- 可复用
- 可演化
- 可被任意上层产品再次调用

的长期资产体系。

## Core Definition

OpenMnemo 开源核心的职责不是直接做成某一个具体产品，而是提供一套稳定的资产内核：

- 统一资产模型：`ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset`
- Git/Gitea 原生存储与版本化
- 记忆树与探针式检索
- FTS + vector + graph + tree/probe 的统一检索层
- 统一导入、提炼、回写能力
- 最小运行时接口，供上层产品调用

## Product Position

OpenMnemo 与 deer-flow 一类系统的关系，定位为：

- OpenMnemo：创造资产内核
- deer-flow 类形态：创造执行层或上层产品形态

OpenMnemo 未来可以衍生出多种上层产品，但开源核心本身不等于其中任意一种产品。

## Open Source Core Boundary

### Must Include

- 资产对象模型与关系模型
- 对话、代码、文档、工作流结果的统一导入
- Git/Gitea 回写与版本化
- 记忆树节点生成、收敛、探针展开
- FTS / vector / graph / probe 统一检索接口
- 技能对象化与来源追踪
- 面向上层产品的最小数据层与运行时接口

### Must Not Rush Into Core

- 完整 deer-flow 式 super agent harness
- 重型多 agent 编排 UI
- 企业级权限、审计、计费
- 插件市场或 skill 市场
- SaaS 托管形态
- 复杂商业授权体系

## Near-term Direction

当前主线应在已有数据层能力基础上继续推进，但目标要从“记忆对象与检索收口”升级为“创造资产内核收口”：

1. 让现有 `memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor` 与三类资产模型对齐
2. 把“记忆树 + 探针检索”升为正式导航层，而不是仅保留为检索策略设想
3. 明确 `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` 的最小对象结构与关系
4. 先以“资产投影/聚合层”承接当前 `DataLayerAPI` 与运行时对象，而不是立即用新资产模型替换 `session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor`
5. 让上层 AI Chat、workflow、agent、workspace 未来都通过该内核回写资产

## Non-goals For Current Stage

- 不在当前阶段把 OpenMnemo 直接做成完整 deer-flow 替代品
- 不在当前阶段优先投入复杂 UI 工作台
- 不在当前阶段优先投入企业协作和商业闭环
- 不在当前阶段把开源核心与某一具体上层应用强绑定

## Success Signal

当 OpenMnemo 开源核心能够稳定回答以下问题时，说明本轮定位升级成立：

- 一个项目最终产出了哪些产品资产？
- 这些资产是通过哪些对话、决策、约束和里程碑逐步形成的？
- 这些过程最终沉淀出了哪些可复用技能？
- 上层任意产品是否都能重新调用这些资产，而不破坏用户对数据的拥有权？

## Change Log

- 2026-03-24: 创建 v003，明确将 OpenMnemo 从“个人 AI 记忆底座”升级定位为“开源创造资产内核”，并正式引入“创作结果 / 创作过程 / 创作能力”三类资产模型作为后续规划主轴。

# Project Todo

## Binding
- Goal version: goal_v003
- Todo version: v003_001
- Created: 2026-03-24
- Updated: 2026-03-24
- Status: active

## Current Focus

本轮进入“创造资产内核”规划收口阶段，优先级如下：

- [ ] 将现有 `memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor` 与 `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` 建立正式映射
- [x] 明确记忆树最小节点层级：`ConversationLeaf / ArchiveBatch / MilestoneNode / StageNode`
- [x] 明确探针式检索最小接口：`probe_main(query, root_ref) / probe_branch(node_id, query?) / probe_leaf(node_id) / hydrate_raw`
- [x] 形成 `Session Tree / Repo Tree / Global Memory Forest / Budget Profiles` 的统一设计稿
- [ ] 将统一设计稿收敛为实现级 schema、storage mapping 与 tool contract
- [ ] 明确三类资产的共享基类字段、索引字段、关系字段、版本字段
- [ ] 明确三类资产模型与当前 `DataLayerAPI` 的兼容桥，避免形成两套平行运行时对象
- [ ] 明确开源核心边界与上层产品边界，避免后续将 deer-flow 类执行层误塞入内核

## Carry-forward Implementation Work

以下工作从 `v002` 继承，仍然有效，未因本轮规划收口而取消：

- [ ] 继续推进提取层从 deterministic baseline 向更强的 structured / LLM extraction 升级
- [ ] 补齐 GiteaAdapter 完整链路验证与配置文档
- [ ] 完成 AI Chat 本地版（Phase 0.5）
- [ ] 完成 AI Chat GitHub Pages 降级版（Phase 0.6）

## Milestones

### Milestone 1：对象模型收口

- [ ] 定义 `BaseAsset`
- [ ] 定义 `ProductAsset`
- [ ] 定义 `MemoryAsset`
- [ ] 定义 `SkillAsset`
- [ ] 定义三类资产之间的关系边

### Milestone 2：导航与检索收口

- [x] 定义记忆树节点结构与收敛策略
- [x] 定义探针式检索路径与命中即止策略
- [x] 将 FTS / vector / graph / probe 纳入统一检索心智模型
- [x] 明确 Git/Gitea 在运行时中是“事实层/版本层”，而不是“运行时记忆树本身”

### Milestone 3：运行时与回写收口

- [ ] 定义资产导入接口
- [ ] 定义资产提炼接口
- [ ] 定义资产回写接口
- [ ] 定义资产投影层如何承接现有 `DataLayerAPI`
- [ ] 定义供上层 AI Chat / workflow / agent 调用的最小运行时接口

### Milestone 4：上层产品分层

- [ ] 明确 deer-flow 类产品在 OpenMnemo 体系中的位置
- [ ] 明确个人工作台、团队协作、企业产品哪些属于上层应用
- [ ] 明确哪些能力进入开源核心，哪些保留给未来商业产品

## Execution Notes

- 当前代码主线仍以既有数据层成果为基础，不推翻已完成的 unit-first retrieval、local runtime、mixed retrieval。
- 本轮优先完成“规划收口”，再决定代码层对象重命名或接口升级节奏。
- 记忆压缩方案继续按照“全量原文保留 + 树形索引收敛 + 探针式逐层展开”原则推进。
- 三类资产统一沉淀到用户自有 Git/Gitea 仓库中，保持 Git-native 定位。
- 当前 `DataLayerAPI` 继续以 `session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed` 作为运行时接口，三类资产模型先作为其上的投影层或聚合层落地。

## Deferred

- [ ] 完整 super agent harness
- [ ] 重型多 agent 编排 UI
- [ ] 企业权限与审计体系
- [ ] 插件市场 / skill 市场
- [ ] SaaS 托管闭环
